perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and it replaces and supersedes all prior agreements between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the complete understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and it replaces and supersedes all prior agreements between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The alteration from 'entire agreement' to 'complete understanding' creates a subtle but important distinction. 'Entire agreement' has a strong legal connotation, signifying that all terms are contained within the document. 'Complete understanding,' however, is more subjective and implies a mutual comprehension, potentially encompassing unwritten understandings or implied terms. This change contradicts the original intention of a fully integrated agreement, as it opens the door for disputes over whether unwritten understandings should be considered part of the contractual obligations, creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the complete understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and it replaces and supersedes all prior agreements between the Parties. No provision of this Agreement may be waived or modified unless such waiver or modification is in writing. No failure or delay by the Company in exercising any right, power or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder. This Agreement may not be assigned by the Recipient, by operation of law or otherwise, without the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement', while the changed text states 'This Agreement constitutes the complete understanding'. The explanation states that the change from 'entire agreement' to 'complete understanding' opens the door for disputes over unwritten understandings, which contradicts the intention of a fully integrated agreement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This means the entire premise of having the contract represents the 'entire agreement' of the parties is jeopardized by the use of the language 'complete understanding', as it implies things outside of the contract may be pertinent.
